assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows mixed results: 2.1.x versions marked YES (affected) and 2.2.x versions marked NO (not affected) based on lock file evidence
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md contains a table with 2.1.0 and 2.1.1 marked YES (h2 0.4.5, below fix threshold 0.4.8) and 2.2.0 through 2.2.4 all marked NO (h2 >= 0.4.8). Notes reference lock file evidence with specific h2 versions at each build tag.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction includes only the actually affected versions (2.1.x versions), not the unaffected 2.2.x versions (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md shows the proposed correction: Current [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.2.0] -> Proposed [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.1.1]. RHTPA 2.2.0 is explicitly removed ('h2 0.4.8 at tag v0.4.5... NOT affected') and RHTPA 2.1.1 is added. Only 2.1.x versions remain in the corrected set.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks are created only for the affected stream (2.1.x), not for 2.2.x which already ships the patched version (Step 8 Case A scoping)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md header states 'Case A -- Affected (create remediation tasks for 2.1.x only)' and explicitly notes 'The 2.2.x stream already ships h2 >= 0.4.8 and requires no remediation.' Both tasks (upstream backport and downstream propagation) target the 2.1.x stream only (release/0.3.z branch, rhtpa-release.0.3.z repo). No 2.2.x tasks exist.

assertion_results.text: The analysis treats the issue as unscoped (no stream suffix) and checks ALL versions across all streams (Step 1 Stream scope resolution, unscoped path)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md states: 'The issue summary... contains no stream suffix in brackets. This issue is therefore unscoped -- it covers all configured version streams.' and 'Both streams will be analyzed in Step 2.' The version-impact.md table includes all 7 versions across both 2.1.x (2 versions) and 2.2.x (5 versions) streams.

assertion_results.text: Cross-stream impact notice is NOT generated because the issue is unscoped — it covers all streams by definition (Step 8 Case B applies only to scoped issues)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md explicitly states: 'Since the issue is unscoped, no cross-stream impact notice (Case B) is generated -- the unscoped issue already covers all streams, so there is no "other stream outside scope" to notify about.'
